description_long: This object consists of two adjacent, pyramid-shaped tents with smooth, white exterior surfaces and angular bases. Each tent features a colored trim along the bottom edge—one with an orange accent and the other with a pink accent—adding a touch of visual distinction. At the apex of each tent, a bundle of brown, stick-like poles protrudes upward, mimicking the appearance of traditional teepee structures. The tents rest on small, black feet at each corner, slightly elevating them above the ground. The surfaces are matte and appear to be made from a synthetic fabric or canvas, supported by internal and external pole structures. No visible doors, windows, or branding are present on the exterior.
description: A pair of white, pyramid-shaped tents with orange and pink trim, each topped with protruding brown poles and resting on small black feet.
description_view_0: Front view showing both tents side by side, with orange trim on the left tent and pink trim on the right, and visible brown poles at the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the pink trim on the nearest tent and the alignment of the brown poles extending upward from both tents.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the pink trim on the left tent and orange trim on the right, with the brown poles still visible at the apex.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the orange trim on the nearest tent and the arrangement of the brown poles at the top.
attribute_entrance_type: No visible entrance; presumed to have a flap or panel on the side not shown in the images.
attribute_guy_line_configuration: No visible guy lines; corners rest on small black feet for stability.
